column1: # Scope: NIS2 risk-management provisions map to ISO 27005 process clauses 5-10.

column9: NIS2 Art.21.1 requires appropriate and proportionate technical, operational and organisational measures to manage risks — ISO 27005 Clause 5 defines the overall risk-management process.
Context establishment (Clause 6) — NIS2 requires determining scope, criteria and stakeholders before control selection.
Risk assessment (Clause 7) — NIS2 Art.21.1 obligation to base measures on an all-hazards risk approach is implemented via 27005 identification/analysis/evaluation.
Risk identification (Clause 7.2) — NIS2 demands identification of risks to network and information systems.
Risk analysis (Clause 7.3) — likelihood and impact evaluation.
Risk evaluation (Clause 7.4) — risks are compared against risk-acceptance criteria.
Risk treatment (Clause 8) — NIS2 proportionate measures are the treatment output; Annex to NIS2 Art.21.2 enumerates control domains.
Residual risk acceptance (Clause 8.3) — management body per NIS2 Art.20 approves residual risk.
Risk management policies — derived from risk treatment process (Clause 8).
Communication and consultation (Clause 9) — policies are communicated across the organisation.
Supply-chain risk identification (Clause 7.2) — covers third-party risk sources.
Monitoring and review (Clause 10) — NIS2 Art.21.2.f effectiveness assessment is the review loop.
Contextual proportionality — NIS2 Art.21.3 exposure/size considerations map to 27005 context establishment.
Management body accountability — Clause 9 communication informs the governance layer required by NIS2 Art.20.
